title: Rustic Turkish Flour Flatbread
description: This sour, crispy flatbread uses strong wholemeal flour and a brine‑wet dough, perfect for a lazy Sunday and dairy‑free friendly.
instructions: In a large bowl whisk water, salt, and olive oil until mixture is slightly frothy.
Gradually sift wholemeal flour into the bowl, stirring until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for about five minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Shape the dough into a round ball and place it on a parchment‑lined tray.
Let the dough rest at room temperature, covered with a damp cloth, for 30 minutes to allow the gluten to relax.
Preheat a cast‑iron skillet over medium‑high heat until hot and add a small splash of oil.
Press the rested dough into a thin disc, about 1/4 inch thick, and transfer it to the skillet.
Cook for 3 to 4 minutes on each side until golden and crispy, then remove, let cool, and serve.
